mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in retail sales signal robust consumer demand and rising corporate revenues, which supports higher equity valuations over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month change on 5-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 4.5%, the tightening of monetary policy compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced risk appetite, leading to negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After the trigger condition is met, more than 50% of subsequent 20-day forward return observations are positive or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous three-month increase in both the consumer price index and the federal funds rate indicates active central bank tightening in response to persistent inflation, which historically depresses equity prices over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: 3-month increase in both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS
failure_condition: After the joint trigger condition, fewer than 55% of 20-day forward returns are negative, or the average return is not statistically negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns, and this negative relationship is most powerful during the late-cycle expansion phase when the labor market is tight and unemployment is below its natural rate.
failure_condition: The conditional Rank IC of UNRATE change on 20-day returns is not more negative in the late-cycle regime than in other regimes, or the unconditional Rank IC is not negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production reflect expanding output and supply-side strength, which tends to support corporate earnings and equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month change on 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 1.2 million units, it signals a robust housing market and residential investment boom, which typically leads to positive equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return after HOUST exceeds 1200 is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the signal generates more than 40% negative instances.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in unemployment and a decrease in retail sales over three months signals a weakening consumer economy and rising recession risk, which reliably depresses equity prices over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rising and RSAFS falling over 3 months
failure_condition: After the joint trigger, fewer than 55% of 20-day forward returns are negative, or the average return is not statistically negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP is a strong positive driver of equity prices during the post-recession recovery phase, as accelerating economic activity lifts corporate earnings and investor sentiment.
failure_condition: During identified recovery phases, the correlation between GDP growth and forward 60-day returns is not positive and significant (p > 0.10), or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over two recovery cycles.
